title: Arrabidaea viscida
taxonomy: Arrabidaea viscida is classified within the family Bignoniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the trumpet creepers or cat's claw vines. This family consists of around 800 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical regions of the world. The Bignoniaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes mint, sage, and lavender. The Lamiales order is part of the larger clade Asterids, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the orders Gentianales and Solanales. The Bignoniaceae family and its related orders are classified within the larger clade Angiosperms, which is the most diverse group of plants on Earth.
